response: {"calibration_score":0.28,"overconfident_count":1,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":1,"biases_detected":["environment_state_assumption — unit-13 proceeded with test spawns assuming ports 5043/5044 were free based on earlier checks; a stale ghost process caused a false-positive action against the live :5042 sys-pier-backend (documented in commit cavaet)","retry_without_root_cause — unit-13 issued 4 successive pkill/kill attempts against the same PID without investigating why the process survived each time before retrying","anchoring_on_prior_state — both unit-7 and unit-13 used port/process state snapshots from earlier in the turn as ground truth for later operations rather than re-verifying immediately before side-effecting spawns"],"recommendations":["Re-verify port occupancy with lsof immediately before each test-server spawn, not at turn start — a single pgrep/lsof takes <100ms and eliminates ghost-process false positives","When pkill/kill fails on the first attempt, inspect with ps -o stat before retrying; a zombie or D-state process requires a different remediation path than a running one","Treat each smoke-test iteration as a clean-slate environment: record spawn PID, confirm the PID is listening on the expected port before issuing curl probes, and kill by recorded PID rather than pattern-matching (avoids hitting surviving ghosts from prior iterations)"]}
